edge: The productionized end-to-end stereo depth sensing system includes pre-processing, on-line stereo rectification, and stereo depth estimation.

The 3D photo quality survey was conducted to understand the quality of the depth system by capturing scenes from smart glasses and rendering novel view videos with depth maps.
The survey involved 24 participants who were asked to rate the quality of the rendered videos on a scale of 1 to 5.
The quality of the depth map does not always directly correlate with the quality of the rendered video, as some scenes with poor depth maps had few artifacts in the rendered view.

In some cases, a poor depth map did not result in artifacts in the rendered view, leading to higher user ratings. However, correct depth maps could still lead to jarring artifacts in the rendered video.
Standard stereo metrics like the percentage of bad pixels can be used to compare the error rates between scenes (10) and (4), indicating that scene (10) likely has a lower error rate.
Evaluating a stereo method's performance solely based on standard metrics may not be sufficient, as demonstrated by the comparison between scenes (10) and (4).
